metadata.description: Portland commercial vacancy rate data from published CRE reports and FRED

sources.name: CBRE Portland MarketView
sources.coverage: Portland metro office and retail vacancy

sources.coverage: US national office vacancy rate (benchmark comparison)

notes: Portland metro office vacancy peaked at ~25% in late 2023/early 2024
CBD (downtown) vacancy significantly higher than metro average, often 5-8pp above
Retail vacancy recovered faster than office, nearing pre-pandemic levels by 2025
Office-to-residential conversions began reducing available supply in 2024
Data sourced from publicly published quarterly market reports

portland_vacancy.notes: CBRE Q1 2019: Portland metro office vacancy. CBD tighter at ~8%. Retail from Colliers.
CBRE Q2 2019: Portland metro office. Strong pre-pandemic market.
CBRE Q3 2019: Portland metro office. Slight uptick but healthy.
CBRE Q4 2019: Portland metro office. Year-end pre-pandemic.
CBRE Q1 2020: Pre-lockdown, slight increase.
CBRE Q2 2020: COVID lockdowns begin. Massive retail impact.
CBRE Q3 2020: Remote work accelerates office vacancy.
CBRE Q4 2020: Portland protests + COVID compound downtown vacancy.
CBRE Q1 2021: Continued WFH. Portland CBD hit harder than suburbs.
CBRE Q2 2021: Vaccines rollout but offices stay remote.
CBRE Q3 2021: Some retail recovery, office continues to rise.
CBRE Q4 2021: Portland metro office vacancy near peak. Retail stabilizing.
Colliers Q1 2022: Portland metro. Omicron delays return-to-office.
Colliers Q2 2022: Portland office vacancy exceeds 20%. CBD worst.
Colliers Q3 2022: CBD vacancy ~25%. Some sublease space returned.
Colliers Q4 2022: Record high. Tech sector contraction (layoffs).
Kidder Mathews Q1 2023: Portland metro. Hybrid work normalizes.
Kidder Mathews Q2 2023: Portland CBD ~28%. Some conversions announced.
JLL Q3 2023: Portland metro office. CBD one of highest in US.
JLL Q4 2023: All-time high. Retail showing resilience downtown.
CBRE Q1 2024: Portland metro. Some sublease absorption.
CBRE Q2 2024: Slight plateau. Office-to-residential conversions underway.
Colliers Q3 2024: First quarterly decline in years. Small but notable.
Colliers Q4 2024: Continued modest improvement. KOIN tower conversion.
Kidder Mathews Q1 2025: Modest continued improvement. CBD still elevated.
Kidder Mathews Q2 2025: Office conversions removing supply. Retail near pre-pandemic.
JLL Q3 2025: Recovery trajectory. Return-to-office mandates helping.
JLL Q4 2025: Best quarter since 2022. Still elevated vs pre-pandemic.
